info.title: Accounting API
info.description:
The Accounting API provides programmatic access to financial data and operations for hospitality properties. Use this API to manage transactions, folios, deposits, accounts receivable ledgers, trial balances, and related accounting configuration.

**Key concepts:**
- **Transactions** represent individual financial entries (charges, payments,
  taxes, fees) associated with a source such as a reservation, group profile,
  or house account.

- **Folios** organize transactions into logical groups within a source. You
  can create custom folios and move or route transactions between them.

- **Source Balances** provide a real-time financial summary (charges, payments,
  balance due, tax/fee breakdowns) for a reservation, group profile, house
  account, or accounts receivable ledger.

- **Accounts Receivable (AR) Ledgers** track outstanding balances that have
  been transferred from reservations or group profiles for later collection.

- **Trial Balance** is a daily financial reconciliation report that summarizes
  ledger activity across guest, deposit, and AR ledgers.

- **Custom Codes** let you map Cloudbeds internal transaction codes to your
  own chart-of-accounts codes and general ledger codes.


**Authentication:**
All endpoints require a valid OAuth2 bearer token. Endpoints are protected by scopes: `read:payment` for read operations and `write:payment` for mutating operations. A few search endpoints use POST with a request body to support complex filter criteria; these require the `read:payment` scope.

**Base URL:**
Use `https://api.cloudbeds.com` for production and `https://api.cloudbeds-stage.com` for staging. All endpoint paths are prefixed with `/accounting/v1.0`.

**Property context:**
Every request must include the `X-Property-ID` header to identify which property the request applies to.

**Monetary amounts:**
Unless otherwise noted, monetary amounts are expressed in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents for USD). Use the `currencyScale` field to determine the number of decimal places.

**Pagination:**
Paginated endpoints return a `nextPageToken` value. Pass this token in subsequent requests to retrieve the next page of results. When `nextPageToken` is null or absent, you have reached the last page.

get.description: Retrieve the accounting settings for a property. Use this endpoint to check the current configuration, such as the deposit consumption policy. Settings control how certain accounting behaviors work across the property.

patch.description: Update one or more accounting settings for a property. You only need to include the fields you want to change; unspecified fields remain unchanged. If no settings exist for the property, they are created automatically.

post.description: Transfer one or more deposit transactions to the guest ledger. Provide the transaction IDs of the deposits you want to transfer. This operation is processed asynchronously; the response includes an event ID you can use to track the result.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of accounts receivable (AR) ledgers for a property. You can filter by status, date range, balance range, profile, and search query. Use AR ledgers to track balances that have been transferred from reservations or group profiles for later collection.

post.description: Create a new accounts receivable (AR) ledger for a property. An AR ledger provides a way to track outstanding balances outside of a reservation, such as invoices sent to a corporate client. You must provide a name, and can optionally link the ledger to a profile.

patch.description: Update an existing accounts receivable (AR) ledger. You can change the name, description, linked profile, or status. Only include the fields you want to update. To close a ledger, set the status to CLOSED; note that a ledger can only be closed when its balance is zero.

get.summary: Get an accounts receivable ledger by ID
get.description: Retrieve a single accounts receivable (AR) ledger by its unique identifier. The response includes the ledger name, status, total charges, amount paid, and outstanding balance.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque token for cursor-based pagination. Pass the `nextPageToken` value from a previous response to retrieve the next page. Omit this parameter (or pass null) for the first page.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page. If not specified, the server uses a default page size.

get.summary: List transactions for an AR ledger
get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of transactions belonging to a specific accounts receivable (AR) ledger. You can filter by transaction state (posted or pending), date ranges, transaction type, and more. Use this endpoint to review the detailed financial activity within an AR ledger.

Retrieve aggregated totals across all accounts receivable (AR) ledgers for a property. The response includes the combined total charges, amount paid, and outstanding balance. You can optionally filter by ledger status to see totals for only open or closed ledgers.

post.summary: Transfer a reservation balance to an AR ledger
post.description: Transfer the outstanding balance of a reservation to an accounts receivable (AR) ledger. This creates a transfer transaction that moves the balance from the reservation to the specified AR ledger for later collection. This operation is processed asynchronously; the response includes an event ID you can use to track the result.

delete.description: Reverse a previously created balance transfer between a reservation and an accounts receivable (AR) ledger. This moves the transferred balance back to the original reservation. This operation is processed asynchronously; the response includes an event ID you can use to track the result.

post.summary: Transfer a group profile balance to an AR ledger
post.description: Transfer the outstanding balance of a group profile to an accounts receivable (AR) ledger. You can optionally specify a folio ID to transfer the balance from a specific folio within the group profile. This operation is processed asynchronously; the response includes an event ID you can use to track the result.

get.summary: Get AR balance transfer details for a reservation
get.description: Retrieve the balance transfer details for a specific reservation. If a balance transfer exists between the reservation and an accounts receivable (AR) ledger, the response includes the transfer transaction ID, amount, and the associated AR ledger ID.

Retrieve all custom general ledger (GL) codes configured for a property. GL codes let you map Cloudbeds transaction categories to your own chart-of-accounts structure for reporting and export to external accounting systems.

put.summary: Create or update custom general ledger codes
put.description: Create or update custom general ledger (GL) codes for a property. Send the full list of GL codes you want to persist. Each code must have a unique name and code value. You can assign codes to groups (payments, products, reservations, taxes/fees, etc.) to categorize them. To archive a code, set its `archived` flag to true.

get.description: Retrieve all custom transaction code mappings for a property. Custom transaction codes let you assign your own codes to each Cloudbeds internal transaction type (e.g., room rate, tax, payment method). You must call the initialize endpoint before codes can be retrieved for the first time.

put.description: Update custom transaction code mappings for a property. For each mapping, provide the record ID and the new custom code value. You can also optionally assign a custom general ledger code to each transaction code. Only the mappings you include in the request are updated; others remain unchanged.

/accounting/v1.0/custom-transaction-code.description: Before transaction custom codes can be retrieved and modified, they must be initialized. This process generates transaction custom code records without any mapping. Once the initialization is complete, the codes can be retrieved and modified.

post.description: Initialize custom transaction code records for a property. You must call this endpoint before you can retrieve or update custom transaction codes. Initialization creates a record for each internal transaction type with an empty custom code mapping. If codes have already been initialized, this endpoint has no additional effect.

Search and retrieve posted transactions for a property. This endpoint uses POST (instead of GET) to support complex filter criteria in the request body. You can combine multiple filters with AND/OR logic, paginate results, and sort by various fields.

**Supported filter fields:**
  - `account_category`
  - `chart_of_account_type`
  - `created_at` (alias for `transaction_datetime`)
  - `customer_id`
  - `custom_code`
  - `external_relation_id`
  - `external_relation_kind`
  - `folio_id`
  - `id`
  - `internal_code`
  - `origin_id`
  - `parent_id`
  - `routed_from`
  - `source_id`
  - `source_identifier`
  - `source_kind`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `trial_balance_id`
  - `service_date`


**Supported sort fields:**
  - `created_at` (alias for `transaction_datetime`)
  - `id`
  - `internal_code`
  - `source_id`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `service_date`


**Required filter constraints:** The API has certain constraints for filters so that the system is able to efficiently query the data. Every request must include at least one of the following base filters:
  - `id` with operator `equals` or `in`
  - `source_id` together with `source_kind`
  - `source_identifier` together with `source_kind`
  - `external_relation_id` together with `external_relation_kind`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `service_date`
  - `trial_balance_id`


**Additional filter rules:**
  - Filters cannot be empty.
  - `source_id` requires `source_kind` to be present in the same request.
  - `source_identifier` requires `source_kind` to be present in the same request.
  - `external_relation_id` and `external_relation_kind` must always be used together.
  - `trial_balance_id` only supports the `equals` operator and cannot appear more than once.
  - When using a single filter condition (without `and`/`or`), only `id`, `transaction_datetime`,
    or `service_date` are allowed, and the operator must be `equals` or `in`.


**Example — single filter condition (no AND/OR needed):**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "field": "id",
      "operator": "in",
      "value": [101, 102, 103]
    }
  }
```

```
  {
    "filters": {
      "field": "service_date",
      "operator": "equals",
      "value": "2024-01-01"
    }
  }
```

**Example — composite filter condition:**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "and": [
        {
          "field": "transaction_datetime",
          "operator": "greater_than_or_equal",
          "value": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
        },
        {
          "field": "transaction_datetime",
          "operator": "less_than",
          "value": "2024-02-01T00:00:00Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
```

**Example — composite filter condition with sorting and pagination:**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "and": [
        {
          "operator": "greater_than_or_equal",
          "value": "2019-01-11T08:59:00Z",
          "field": "transaction_datetime"
        },
        {
          "operator": "equals",
          "value": "123",
          "field": "source_id"
        },
        {
          "operator": "equals",
          "value": "RESERVATION",
          "field": "source_kind"
        },
        {
          "or": [
            {
              "operator": "in",
              "value": ["1", "2", "3"],
              "field": "customer_id"
            },
            {
              "operator": "equals",
              "value": "9000",
              "field": "internal_code"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "pageToken": null,
    "limit": 10,
    "sort": [
      {
        "field": "transaction_datetime",
        "direction": "asc"
      }
    ]
  }
```

get.description: Retrieve all folios for a given source (reservation, group profile, or configuration). Folios organize transactions into logical groups, such as separating room charges from incidentals. The filter requires both a sourceId and sourceKind to identify the source.

post.description: Create a new folio for a source (reservation, group profile, or configuration). You must provide a name, the source details, and the transaction types assigned to it.

parameters.name: propagateDownstream
parameters.description: When true and source kind is CONFIG, create matching child folios for active reservations and groups using this configuration.

put.description: Update an existing folio. You can change the folio name and the transaction types assigned to it. Transaction types control which kinds of transactions are automatically routed to this folio. Other folio properties (source, configuration) cannot be changed after creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Folio ID

parameters.name: propagateDownstream
parameters.description: When true and the folio source kind is CONFIG, propagate name and transaction type changes to child folios of active reservations and groups using this configuration.

delete.description: Delete a folio. The folio is soft-deleted and will no longer appear in listings. You cannot delete a folio that still has transactions assigned to it; move or unroute all transactions before deleting.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Folio ID

post.description: Move transactions to a different folio within the same source. You can specify individual transaction IDs or transaction types (by origin ID and external relation kind) to move all matching transactions at once. At least one of `transactions` or `transactionTypes` must be provided.

post.summary: Route transactions to a group profile
post.description: Route reservation transactions to a group profile folio. Routing binds reservation transactions to a group profile and assigns them to the specified folio. You can specify individual transaction IDs or transaction types (by origin ID and external relation kind) to route all matching transactions at once. At least one of `transactions` or `transactionTypes` must be provided.

post.summary: Unroute transactions from a group profile
post.description: Unroute transactions from a group profile back to the original reservation. This reverses a previous routing operation, removing the transactions from the group profile and returning them to the reservation they originated from. Unlike move and route operations, only individual transaction IDs are supported (transaction types are not accepted).

Search and retrieve pending (not yet posted) transactions for a property. Pending transactions represent charges, payments, or adjustments that have been created but not yet finalized. This endpoint uses POST (instead of GET) to support complex filter criteria in the request body. The filter and sort options are the same as for posted transactions.

**Supported filter fields:**
  - `account_category`
  - `chart_of_account_type`
  - `created_at` (alias for `transaction_datetime`)
  - `customer_id`
  - `custom_code`
  - `external_relation_id`
  - `external_relation_kind`
  - `folio_id`
  - `id`
  - `internal_code`
  - `origin_id`
  - `parent_id`
  - `routed_from`
  - `source_id`
  - `source_identifier`
  - `source_kind`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `trial_balance_id`
  - `service_date`


**Supported sort fields:**
  - `created_at` (alias for `transaction_datetime`)
  - `id`
  - `internal_code`
  - `source_id`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `service_date`


**Required filter constraints:** The API has certain constraints for filters so that the system is able to efficiently query the data. Every request must include at least one of the following base filters:
  - `id` with operator `equals` or `in`
  - `source_id` together with `source_kind`
  - `source_identifier` together with `source_kind`
  - `external_relation_id` together with `external_relation_kind`
  - `transaction_datetime`
  - `service_date`
  - `trial_balance_id`


**Additional filter rules:**
  - Filters cannot be empty.
  - `source_id` requires `source_kind` to be present in the same request.
  - `source_identifier` requires `source_kind` to be present in the same request.
  - `external_relation_id` and `external_relation_kind` must always be used together.
  - `trial_balance_id` only supports the `equals` operator and cannot appear more than once.
  - When using a single filter condition (without `and`/`or`), only `id`, `transaction_datetime`,
    or `service_date` are allowed, and the operator must be `equals` or `in`.


**Example — single filter condition (no AND/OR needed):**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "field": "id",
      "operator": "in",
      "value": [101, 102, 103]
    }
  }
```

```
  {
    "filters": {
      "field": "service_date",
      "operator": "equals",
      "value": "2024-01-01"
    }
  }
```

**Example — composite filter condition:**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "and": [
        {
          "field": "transaction_datetime",
          "operator": "greater_than_or_equal",
          "value": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
        },
        {
          "field": "transaction_datetime",
          "operator": "less_than",
          "value": "2024-02-01T00:00:00Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
```

**Example — composite filter condition with sorting and pagination:**
```
  {
    "filters": {
      "and": [
        {
          "operator": "greater_than_or_equal",
          "value": "2019-01-11T08:59:00Z",
          "field": "transaction_datetime"
        },
        {
          "operator": "equals",
          "value": "123",
          "field": "source_id"
        },
        {
          "operator": "equals",
          "value": "RESERVATION",
          "field": "source_kind"
        },
        {
          "or": [
            {
              "operator": "in",
              "value": ["1", "2", "3"],
              "field": "customer_id"
            },
            {
              "operator": "equals",
              "value": "9000",
              "field": "internal_code"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "pageToken": null,
    "limit": 10,
    "sort": [
      {
        "field": "transaction_datetime",
        "direction": "asc"
      }
    ]
  }
```

Search and retrieve both posted and pending transactions for a source (reservation, group profile, or house account), merged into a single response. This endpoint uses POST (instead of GET) to support complex filter and grouping criteria in the request body. Results can be grouped by date, transaction type, folio, or other fields.

Results are always wrapped in groups. When groupBy is specified, transactions are grouped by that field. When omitted, all transactions are placed in a single group with key "default". Pagination is applied first (cursor-based on flat transactions), then grouping is applied to the page results. Groups at page boundaries may be partial.

When includeTotal is true, totals and foreign currency totals are computed on the first page and cached in Redis. Subsequent pages return cached totals. When no filters are applied, the total amount is read from the pre-computed source balance for optimal performance.

Supported filter fields: folioId, posted, descriptionFilters, transactionDate range, serviceDate range, subSourceIds, searchQuery.

Supported sort fields: transaction_datetime, service_date, id, internal_code.

Supported groupBy fields: transaction_date, service_date, internal_code_group, description, sub_source_identifier, folio_id, user_id.

get.description: Retrieve a summary of each folio for a given source (reservation or group profile). Returns a map keyed by folio ID, where each value contains the folio's balance due. The balance is computed from both posted and pending transactions assigned to each folio.

Check whether a property has configured its trial balance. The response indicates whether the trial balance is configured and, if so, the date it was set up. You must configure the trial balance before you can generate daily reports.

post.description: Calculate the initial trial balance for a property based on all historical transaction records up to the end of yesterday (in property local time). Use this endpoint to preview the opening balances before committing the trial balance configuration. The response contains the computed deposit ledger, accounts receivable ledger, and guest ledger opening balances.

get.description: Retrieve the saved trial balance configuration for a property, including the configured opening balances for the deposit ledger, accounts receivable ledger, and guest ledger. Returns the configuration date and the opening balance values that were set when the trial balance was initialized.

post.description: Configure the trial balance for a property by saving the opening balances as of today. You must provide opening balances for the deposit ledger, accounts receivable ledger, and guest ledger. This operation can only be performed once per property; it returns an error if a trial balance configuration already exists. Use the calculate endpoint first to preview the recommended opening balance values.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Identifier of the source to get the deposit balance for (e.g., a reservation ID). Omit along with sourceKind to get the property-wide deposit balance.

parameters.name: sourceKind
parameters.description: Type of the source entity. Required when sourceId is provided.

get.description: Retrieve the deposit balance for a specific source or the entire property. To get the balance for a specific reservation or other source, provide both sourceId and sourceKind. To get the aggregate deposit balance for the whole property, omit both parameters. The response includes the posted balance and pending balance in the smallest currency unit.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque token for cursor-based pagination. Pass the `nextPageToken` value from a previous response to retrieve the next page. Omit this parameter (or pass null) for the first page.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page. If not specified, the server uses a default page size.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of deposit transactions for a property. You can filter by transaction state, reservation, date ranges (transaction date, check-in, check-out), reservation status, and user. Use this endpoint to review deposit activity including payments received, transfers, and refunds.

Retrieve the trial balance report for a specific date. The report provides a daily financial reconciliation showing opening and closing balances, ledger activity, deposit and AR transfers, and a detailed breakdown of guest ledger charges, taxes, and payments by transaction code. The property must have a configured trial balance before reports can be generated.

Retrieve the complete list of Cloudbeds internal transaction codes. These are system-defined codes that categorize each type of transaction (e.g., room rate, tax, fee, payment). Internal codes cannot be modified, but you can map them to your own custom codes using the custom transaction codes endpoints.

Retrieve the financial balance summary for a specific source. The source can be a reservation, house account, group profile, or accounts receivable ledger, identified by the sourceKind and sourceId path parameters. The response includes a detailed breakdown of charges (subtotal, additional items, taxes, fees), payments, refunds, upcoming payments, and the overall balance due. Use the optional Accept-Language header to receive localized tax and fee names.
Get source balance snapshot information for a reservation

parameters.name: includeFolios
parameters.description: Whether to include folios and their transaction types

get.description: Get all folio configurations for a property

parameters.name: propagateDownstream
parameters.description: Whether to propagate changes to downstream reservation and group folios

put.summary: Create or update a folio configuration
put.description: Create a new folio configuration or update an existing one for a property. A folio configuration defines a template for how folios are structured, including which booking sources are included and which transaction types are assigned to each folio. To update an existing configuration, include its ID in the request body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the folio configuration

patch.description: Partially update a folio configuration. Accepts optional isDefault, name, and description fields. Only provided fields are updated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the folio configuration

parameters.name: includeFolios
parameters.description: Whether to include folios and their transaction types

get.description: Get a folio configuration by ID

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the folio configuration

delete.description: Delete a folio configuration by its ID. The default configuration cannot be deleted; set another configuration as default first. Existing folios that were created from this configuration are not affected.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Source ID (e.g., Reservation ID, Group Profile ID)

get.description: Get which folio configuration is assigned to a source

put.description: Assign a folio configuration to a source, replacing existing folios

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Group Profile ID

parameters.name: sourceKind
parameters.description: Deprecated. Defaults to GROUP_PROFILE if not provided.

parameters.name: subSourceId
parameters.description: Allotment Block ID. When provided, filters rules to only that block. When omitted, returns only group-level rules unless includeSubSourceRules is true.

parameters.name: includeSubSourceRules
parameters.description: When true and subSourceId is omitted, also includes block-level (sub-source) rules in the response. Ignored when subSourceId is provided.

get.description: Retrieve the autorouting rules configured for a group profile. Autorouting rules define which transaction types are automatically routed from reservations to a group profile folio.

post.description: Create a new autorouting rule for a group profile. The rule defines which transaction types are automatically routed from reservations to a group profile folio.

put.description: Update or create autorouting rules for a group profile. Entries with an id are updated; entries without an id are created as new rules.

get.summary: Get the structured filter tree of routable transaction types
get.description: Returns the debit and credit filter tree the folio-config UI uses to pick transaction types to route into a folio. The tree is composed server-side and returned ready to render; the client does not assemble any nodes itself. Labels are localized via the Accept-Language header.

scopes.write:payment: Create, update, or delete payment and transaction data

includeInactive.description: Include inactive transactions (voided, consumed, cancelled, deleted)

sourceId.description: Filter by source ID. Must be used with sourceKind.

sourceKind.description: Filter by source kind. Must be used with sourceId.

reservationStatuses.description: Filter by one or more reservation statuses (OR logic). When provided, the deprecated reservationStatus field is ignored.

propertyIdHeader.name: X-Property-ID
propertyIdHeader.description: Unique identifier of the property. Required for all requests to scope data to a specific property.

accountsReceivableLedgerId.name: accountsReceivableLedgerId
accountsReceivableLedgerId.description: Unique identifier of the accounts receivable ledger.

sourceId.name: sourceId
sourceId.description: Unique identifier of the source entity. The meaning depends on the sourceKind parameter: a reservation ID, house account ID, group profile ID, or accounts receivable ledger ID.

sourceKind.name: sourceKind
sourceKind.description: The type of source entity. Determines how the sourceId is interpreted.

acceptLanguageHeader.name: Accept-Language
acceptLanguageHeader.description:
Language preference for localized tax/fee names.
Supports: en-US (English US), es-ES (Spanish Spain), es-MX (Spanish Mexico), 
fr-FR (French), de-DE (German), it-IT (Italian), pt-PT (Portuguese Portugal), pt-BR (Portuguese Brazil).
Defaults to en-US if not provided or unsupported language is specified.

TransactionItemGroup.description:
Category grouping for transaction item codes. Used to classify custom transaction codes and general ledger codes.
- `items_services` — Products and services sold to guests (minibar, restaurant, spa, etc.).
- `reservations` — Room rate charges and room revenue adjustments.
- `taxes_fees` — Tax and fee line items applied to charges.
- `payments` — Payment transactions (cash, card, bank transfer, etc.).
- `accrual_accounting` — Accrual accounting entries for revenue recognition.
- `custom_item` — Custom POS or manually created charge items.
- `spaces` — Charges related to bookable spaces (meeting rooms, event venues, etc.).
- `accounts_receivable_transfers` — Transfers to or from accounts receivable ledgers.
- `deposit_transfers` — Transfers to or from the deposit ledger.

DepositConsumptionEnum.description: Controls when deposit transactions are automatically transferred to the guest ledger. MANUAL means deposits are only transferred when explicitly requested. CHECK_IN means deposits are automatically transferred when the guest checks in. CHECK_OUT means deposits are automatically transferred when the guest checks out.

AnyValue.description: Can be any of supported OpenApi types.

AccountsReceivableLedgerStatus.description: Status of an accounts receivable ledger. OPEN ledgers can receive balance transfers and payments. CLOSED ledgers are settled and no longer accept new transactions.

TransactionState.description: The state of a transaction. POSTED transactions are finalized and appear on the guest folio. PENDING transactions have been created but are not yet finalized (e.g., an authorized payment that has not been captured).

ApiErrorCode.description:
Error code indicating the type of failure. Use this value for programmatic error handling.
- `UNKNOWN_ERROR` — An unclassified error occurred. Contact support if this persists.
- `UNEXPECTED_ERROR` — An unexpected internal error occurred. Retry the request or contact support.
- `ACCESS_DENIED` — You do not have permission to perform this operation on the specified property.
- `INVALID_REQUEST` — The request body or parameters are invalid. Check the errorDetails field for specifics.
- `NOT_UNIQUE_VALUE` — A value that must be unique (such as a name or code) already exists.
- `INVALID_USER_INFORMATION` — The user information provided is invalid or could not be resolved.
- `ENTITY_NOT_FOUND` — The requested entity was not found.
- `ACCOUNTS_RECEIVABLE_LEDGER_FEATURE_DISABLED` — The accounts receivable ledger feature is not enabled for this property.
- `BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` — The specified reservation or booking was not found.
- `ACCOUNTS_RECEIVABLE_LEDGER_NOT_FOUND` — The specified accounts receivable ledger was not found.
- `ACCOUNTS_RECEIVABLE_LEDGER_STATUS_ERROR` — The AR ledger is in a status that does not allow this operation (e.g., attempting to transfer to a closed ledger).
- `BOOKING_STATUS_ERROR` — The reservation is in a status that does not allow this operation.
- `GROUP_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND` — The specified group profile was not found.
- `GROUP_PROFILE_STATUS_ERROR` — The group profile is in a status that does not allow this operation.
- `GROUP_PROFILE_FOLIO_ERROR` — An error occurred with the group profile folio (e.g., the specified folio does not belong to the group profile).
- `DEPOSIT_TRANSACTION_CAN_NOT_BE_TRANSFERRED` — The deposit transaction cannot be transferred.
- `INVALID_ARGUMENT_ERROR` — A provided argument is invalid (e.g., an unrecognized filter or sort field). Check the errorDetails field for specifics.
- `FOLIO_HAS_TRANSACTIONS` — The folio cannot be deleted because it has transactions assigned to it. Move or unroute the transactions first.
- `SOURCE_HAS_ROUTED_TRANSACTIONS` — The source's folio configuration cannot be changed because it has routed transactions involving its folios (transactions originating from a reservation source, or routed onto a group source). Unroute the transactions first.

sortDirection.description: Direction for sort - asc or desc

Actions.description: Returns the list of actions available for the transaction

parameters.description: Parameters required for the action, can be any object.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

type.description: REAL_USER - user was found, UNKNOWN_USER - user was not found, SYSTEM_USER - user_id = 0 OR null

code.description: The custom code assigned by the user.

transactionItemCode.description: The system code that cannot be modified.

posItemId.description: POS Item ID for items_services group

channelId.description: Channel ID for channel commission group

MoveTransactionsRequest.description: Request to move transactions to a target folio. At least one of `transactions` or `transactionTypes` must be provided and non-empty.

sourceId.description: Source ID (reservation ID, group profile ID, etc.)

targetFolioId.description: Target folio ID where transactions will be moved

transactions.description: List of transaction IDs to move. Required if transactionTypes is not provided.

transactionTypes.description: List of transaction types to move (by origin ID and external relation kind). Required if transactions is not provided.

RouteTransactionsRequest.description: Request to route reservation transactions to a group profile folio. At least one of `transactions` or `transactionTypes` must be provided and non-empty. Route operation binds reservation transactions to a group profile.

reservationId.description: Reservation ID (source of the transactions to route)

groupProfileId.description: Group profile ID (target of the routing)

targetFolioId.description: Target folio ID where transactions will be assigned

transactions.description: List of transaction IDs to route. Required if transactionTypes is not provided.

transactionTypes.description: List of transaction types to route (by origin ID and external relation kind). Required if transactions is not provided.

UnrouteTransactionsRequest.description: Request to unroute transactions from a group profile back to the original reservation. Only transaction IDs are supported (no transaction types).

transactions.description: List of transaction IDs to unroute from the group profile.

originId.description: Origin ID of the transaction type

RouteChildrenType.description: Child transaction types that can follow their parent to the same target folio. Only TAX and FEE are supported.

limit.description: Optional parameter. Default value is 100, maximum value is 1100.
Optional parameter. Default value is 100, maximum value is 1100.

profileId.description: Profile ID associated with this accounts receivable ledger

total.description: Total amount of all charges transferred to this AR ledger, in the smallest currency unit.

paid.description: Total amount of payments applied to this AR ledger, in the smallest currency unit.

balance.description: Outstanding balance (total minus paid), in the smallest currency unit.

propertyId.description: Property ID where the transaction was created.

internalTransactionCode.description: Internal code for the transaction, managed by Cloudbeds.

customTransactionCode.description: Custom code for the transaction, managed by Property.

generalLedgerCustomCode.description: Custom code for general ledger, managed by Property.

currencyScale.description: Number of decimal places for the currency.

currency.description: Currency (ISO code) applied to the amount of the transaction.

customerId.description: Id of the user who perform the transaction, also know as guest id.

rootId.description: Root Id of the transaction, it contains the id of the transaction that is related to it.

parentId.description: Id of the transaction that is parent of this one. For example Tax on top of a rate, tax on top of a fee, etc.

sourceId.description: Id of the source. It is related with the source_kind, so if sourceKind is RESERVATION, is the reservation id.

subSourceId.description: Id of the sub source. At the moment only for reservations that is the booking_room_id.

externalRelationId.description: External relation id, for example if the transaction is a payment it will contain payment id.

externalRelationKind.description: Kind of the external relation id, for example if transaction is a payment it will contain PAYMENT.

originId.description: Id of origin of the transaction. For example if the transaction is created based on a rate, is the rate id.

routedFrom.description: Id of the transaction that was routed from. It can be null.

userId.description: ID of the user who created the transaction

sourceDatetime.description: Date time the source was created. (ISO 8601) in UTC

transactionDatetime.description: Date time when the transaction should be created at. (ISO 8601) in UTC

transactionDatetimePropertyTime.description: Date time when the transaction should be created at base on the property timezone.

serviceDate.description: Date when the posted transaction was created (property time).

createdAt.description: Date time when the transaction was inserted on the database. (ISO 8601) in UTC

sourceIdentifier.description: If source_kind = RESERVATION, this field will contain a reservation identifier. For a transaction with source_kind = GROUP_PROFILE, this field will contain a group code. For source_king = HOUSE_ACCOUNT it will be null.

transferredAmount.description: Total amount already transferred to guest ledger. Null for non-deposit or inactive transactions.

refundedAmount.description: Total amount already refunded via payment service. Null for non-deposit or inactive transactions.

availableAmount.description: Amount still available for transfer or refund. Null for non-deposit or inactive transactions.

currencyScale.description: Number of decimal places for the currency.

posted.description: Flag to mark if transaction is posted

currencyScale.description: Number of decimal places for the currency.

posted.description: Flag to mark if transaction is posted

transferDetails.description: Reservation or Group Profile details for which the transfer was made.

DepositSource.description: Source entity details. Exactly one of the nested objects will be present.

postedBalance.description: Posted balance in smallest unit according to the currency.

pendingBalance.description: Pending balance in smallest unit according to the currency.

AccountsReceivableLedgerTotalsResponse.description: Aggregated totals across all matching accounts receivable ledgers.

total.description: Sum of all charges across matching AR ledgers, in the smallest currency unit.

paid.description: Sum of all payments across matching AR ledgers, in the smallest currency unit.

balance.description: Sum of outstanding balances across matching AR ledgers (total minus paid), in the smallest currency unit.

items.description: List of transaction ids for transferring a deposit

amount.description: Partial amount to transfer in decimal form (e.g. 50.00). When provided, transaction_ids must contain exactly one element. When omitted, full amount of each transaction is transferred.

eventId.description: Id of event which will be processed asynchronous.

currencyScale.description: Number of decimal places for the currency

grandTotal.description: Grand total amount in smallest currency unit

subtotal.description: Subtotal amount in smallest currency unit

additionalItems.description: Additional items amount in smallest currency unit

arTransfer.description: Accounts receivable transfer amount in smallest currency unit

amountPaid.description: Amount paid in smallest currency unit

refundIssued.description: Refund issued amount in smallest currency unit

upcomingPayments.description: Upcoming payments amount in smallest currency unit

upcomingRefund.description: Upcoming refund amount in smallest currency unit

cancellationFee.description: Cancellation fee amount in smallest currency unit

noShowFee.description: No show fee amount in smallest currency unit

taxBreakdown.description: Tax breakdown details as array of items with id and amount

feeBreakdown.description: Fee breakdown details as array of items with id and amount

balanceDue.description: Balance due amount in smallest currency unit

channelCommission.description: Channel commission amount in smallest currency unit

gratuity.description: Gratuity (TIP) total in smallest currency unit (ITC 8995 family).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the balance was last updated (UTC)

BalanceBreakdownItem.description: Breakdown item with name and amount for tax or fee breakdown

name.description: The name of the breakdown item (e.g., tax name, fee name)

amount.description: The amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents for USD)

SourceKind.description: The type of financial source that owns transactions and balances. RESERVATION is an individual guest booking, HOUSE_ACCOUNT is a property-level account for miscellaneous charges, GROUP_PROFILE is a group booking that can aggregate charges from multiple reservations, and ACCOUNTS_RECEIVABLE_LEDGER tracks balances transferred for later collection.

subSourceId.description: Allotment Block ID. Null for group-level rules.

rules.description: Rule entries. For group-level rules this contains exactly one entry (no folioId). For block-level rules each entry targets a different folio.

enabled.description: Whether this rule entry is enabled

folioId.description: Target folio ID for this entry

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that follow their parent to the same folio

originId.description: Origin ID for the transaction type (e.g., fee ID, tax ID)
Origin ID for the transaction type (e.g., fee ID, tax ID)

enabled.description: Whether this rule entry is enabled

folioId.description: Target folio ID. Required for block-level rule entries (when the outer subSourceId is set). Must be omitted for group-level rule entries.

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that should follow their parent to the same folio

transactionTypes.description: Transaction types that this entry routes

subSourceId.description: Allotment Block ID. Null for group-level rules, required for block-level rules.

rules.description: Rule entries. For group-level rules provide exactly one entry (no folioId). For block-level rules provide one entry per target folio.

id.description: ID of the rule entry to update. When omitted, a new rule entry is created (sourceId and subSourceId must be provided on the parent request in that case).

enabled.description: Whether this rule entry is enabled

folioId.description: Target folio ID. For block-level rules only. When omitted the existing folio is preserved (partial-update semantics).

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that should follow their parent to the same folio

transactionTypes.description: Transaction types that this entry routes

sourceId.description: Group Profile ID. Required when any entry in the rules array omits an id (i.e. is a new entry to create).

subSourceId.description: Allotment Block ID. Required for block-level rules when creating new entries.

rules.description: Rule entries to create or update. Entries with an id are updated; entries without an id are created as new rules.

configured.description: True if this property has configured trial balance.

guestLedgerActivity.description: Net activity in the guest ledger for the day.

totalActivity.description: Sum of all posted transactions of the day across the 3 ledgers.

guestLedgerOpeningBalance.description: Guest ledger opening balance for the day.

guestLedgerTransactionsTotal.description: Total of guest ledger transactions posted on the day (excludes transfers).

guestLedgerDepositTransfers.description: Deposit transfers posted into the guest ledger on the day.

guestLedgerArTransfers.description: AR transfers posted out of the guest ledger on the day.

guestLedgerClosingBalance.description: Guest ledger closing balance for the day.

ReservationStatus.description: Current status of a reservation. The status affects which accounting operations are permitted (e.g., charges cannot be added to a checked-out reservation).

NextPageToken.description: Token for fetching the next page of results

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that follow their parent to this folio
Child transaction types that follow their parent to this folio

sourceId.description: Source ID (reservation ID, group profile ID, etc.)

FolioTransactionsFiltersResponse.description: Structured filter tree of routable transaction types. The `debits` and `credits` arrays are independent top-level groups; each renders as its own tree. Nodes are recursive — leaf nodes are the ones a user selects to route into a folio.

FolioFilterGroup.description: A node in the filter tree. Either a parent (with `children`) or a leaf (with `kind` + `originId`). Parent nodes are groupings and are not selectable themselves.

id.description: Stable identifier within the tree. For leaf nodes this IS the transaction-type identifier persisted on folio configurations (e.g. `tax_5`, `product_3`, `roomRevenue_manual`, `cards`, `accountsReceivable`) — save it as-is when assigning transaction types to a folio; no client-side mapping is needed. Group nodes use synthetic `group:*` / `category:*` ids and are not selectable.

label.description: Localized display text. May carry a suffix such as "(deleted)" or "(disabled)".

externalRelationKind.description: Leaf nodes only — the transaction category this node routes. Absent on group nodes.

originId.description: Leaf nodes only — the identifier of the underlying entity (tax id, product id, addon id, payment method code, …). String because some kinds use non-numeric codes.

active.description: False when the underlying entry is disabled.

deleted.description: True when the underlying entry is soft-deleted or past `expiredOn`. Still listed so historical transactions can be routed.

expiredOn.description: Taxes/fees only — the moment the entry stops applying.

FolioFilterNodeKind.description: Role of a node in the tree. `GROUP_*` values are container nodes that hold `children`; every other value identifies a leaf node category.

sourceId.description: Source ID (reservation ID or group profile ID)

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that follow their parent to this folio

transactionTypes.description: Transaction types to assign to this folio

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that follow their parent to this folio

transactionTypes.description: Transaction types to assign to this folio

folioConfigurationId.description: Deprecated. Use configurationId instead. Folio configuration ID.

parentId.description: Parent folio ID (null for root folios)

sourceId.description: Source ID (reservation ID, group profile ID, or config ID)

routeChildrenTypes.description: Child transaction types that follow their parent to this folio. Reflects the folio's current value — set manually via POST/PUT or inherited from the configuration template via propagation.

transactionTypes.description: Transaction types assigned to this folio

originId.description: Origin ID of the transaction type

FolioSourceKind.description: The type of source a folio belongs to. CONFIG indicates a folio configuration template, RESERVATION is an individual guest booking, and GROUP_PROFILE is a group booking profile.

RoutingRuleSourceKind.description: Deprecated. Defaults to GROUP_PROFILE if not provided.

sourceId.description: Source ID (reservation ID, group profile ID, house account ID)

posted.description: Filter by posted status. Omit to include both posted and pending.

descriptionFilters.description: Filter by transaction description identifiers. Accepts composite identifiers in the format: "{type}_{originId}" for item-level filtering (e.g. "product_123", "tax_456", "addon_789", "fee_101", "custom_item_202", "bookable_resource_303"), literal group names for room revenue ("rate", "roomRevenue_manual", "roomRevenue_cancellation", "roomRevenue_no_show", "accountsReceivable"), or payment method codes ("cash", "ebanking", "cards_visa", "cards_master", "pay_pal"). Server translates these to internalTransactionCode group + originId query conditions.

transactionDateFrom.description: Filter transactions from this datetime (UTC, inclusive)

transactionDateTo.description: Filter transactions to this datetime (UTC, inclusive)

subSourceIds.description: Filter by sub-source IDs (booking room IDs for reservations)

searchQuery.description: Free text search on transaction description

includeVoided.description: Include voided transactions. When false, both void transactions and the original transactions they voided are excluded (full chain removal via rootId matching).

includeTotal.description: Include totals in response. On first page, totals are computed and cached in Redis. Subsequent pages return cached totals. When no filters are applied, total amount is read from the pre-computed source balance.

FolioTransactionGroupByField.description: Field to group transactions by. When omitted, all transactions are placed in a single group with key "default".

groups.description: Ordered map of groupKey to group. Key is the string value of the groupBy field (e.g. "2026-02-25" for transaction_date). Pagination is applied first, then grouping.

totals.description: Present only when includeTotal=true. Covers all matching transactions across all pages.

foreignCurrencyTotals.description: Present only when includeTotal=true. Covers all matching transactions across all pages.

totals.description: Per-group totals for the items in the current page only.

amount.description: Transaction amount in decimal form (e.g. 150.00, not 15000). Sign depends on account type.

currencyScale.description: Number of decimal places for the currency

transactionType.description: UI-facing transaction type. Derived from internalTransactionCode: if code suffix is "A" → "adjustment", otherwise mapped from internalCodeGroup. Maps 1:1 to MFD ReservationTransactionType except roomRevenue is split into 3.

internalCodeGroup.description: Coarse transaction type group derived from internalTransactionCode (e.g. PAYMENT, TAX, ITEM_SERVICE).

readOnly.description: Whether the transaction is read-only (no void/refund/allocation actions allowed). Computed from reservation status, property config, and transaction state.

roomName.description: Room name/identifier for the booking room associated with this transaction

actions.description: Returns the list of actions available for the transaction

locked.description: Whether transaction is locked by fiscal documents

allocatedPayments.description: Payment allocations for payment-type transactions. Only present for PAYMENT transactions.

foreignCurrency.description: Original payment currency code when different from property currency

allocatedAmount.description: Amount allocated in smallest currency unit

transactionId.description: ID of the transaction (invoice/expense) this payment is allocated to

totalToAllocate.description: Total amount of the allocated transaction in smallest currency unit

FolioTransactionTotals.description: Global totals across all matching transactions (all pages).

totalDebit.description: Sum of all debit amounts in smallest currency unit

totalCredit.description: Sum of all credit amounts in smallest currency unit

FolioTransactionGroupTotals.description: Per-group totals for the current page only.

ForeignCurrencyTotals.description: Multi-currency totals across all matching transactions (all pages).

debit.description: Map of currency code to total debit amount (e.g. {"RON": 724.82, "EUR": 5})

credit.description: Map of currency code to total credit amount

PaymentDetails.description: Payment-specific fields. Only present on transactions where internalCodeGroup=PAYMENT. Enriched via batch PaymentService lookup.

paymentId.description: Payment ID from the payment service

cardTrailingDigits.description: Last 4 digits of the card number

RoutingContext.description: Routing context info. Present when the transaction is involved in routing — either routed from another source (routedFrom is set) or routed to another source.

FolioSummary.description: Summary data for a single folio.
